---
title: "Klima Protocol — Fee vs. Principal"
subtitle: "Revenue and risk for a carbon-retirement intermediary"
---

```{=html}
<style>
  /* Drawable custom-curve widget — landing page only. */
  .pc-curve-box { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa; padding: 0.75rem 1rem 1rem; margin: 0.75rem 0; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.4rem; margin: 0 0 0.6rem; align-items: center; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button { padding: 0.3rem 0.7rem; border: 1px solid #888; background: #fff; color: #111; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 500; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button:hover { background: #eef; border-color: #555; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar .hint { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #666; margin-left: auto; }
  .pc-curve-canvas { display: block; max-width: 100%; height: auto; touch-action: none; cursor: crosshair; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; }
</style>
```

A carbon-retirement intermediary buys carbon tokens (kVCM) on the open
market and burns them to retire carbon credits on behalf of clients.
It can charge a **fee** — a markup on the live token price — or quote
a **fixed USD price** and carry the inventory risk itself. This site
quantifies the revenue and risk each choice produces, and how the
intermediary can reshape the risk once committed.

The headline answer: a fixed quote that matches the fee book's
expected revenue does *not* match its risk. The principal book trades
that mean preservation for a left-skewed loss tail — but the tail is
shapeable, first by pre-buying inventory and syndicating a slice, then
(decisively) by holding the option to flip back to fee pricing if the
token rallies.

## Findings

1. **Matching means does not match distributions.** Set the fixed
   quote to $Q^* = (1+f) \cdot P \cdot S_0 \cdot (e^{\mu T}-1)/(\mu
   T)$ and the fee and back-to-back principal books have identical
   $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$. Their distributions are near-mirror images,
   however: both are driven by the shared kernel $I_T = \int_0^T S_t\,
   dt$, entered with a plus sign in the fee book (bounded below by
   zero) and a minus sign in the principal book (upside capped at $Q
   \cdot N$, downside unbounded). Picking $Q = Q^*$ trades mean
   preservation for a left-skewed loss tail. This is the programme's
   central asymmetry.

2. **Hedging the principal book is a linear dial.** Pre-buying a
   fraction $\alpha$ of inventory collapses the variance as
   $(1-\alpha)^2$ and so collapses SD and CVaR linearly: doubling
   coverage roughly halves the tail loss. Raising the cost basis
   $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ on that inventory translates the distribution
   leftward by the same amount but does *not* touch SD, VaR, or CVaR —
   basis is a sunk cost. Risk managers should watch coverage, not
   unrealised gain or loss on inventory.

3. **A switch truncates the tail rather than rescaling it.**
   Pre-buying (partial variant) and quota-share syndication multiply the
   left tail by constants — they shrink it but cannot bound it. If
   the intermediary quotes $Q$ up front but keeps the option to flip
   to fee pricing the first time $S_t$ crosses an upper barrier $H =
   h \cdot S_0$ (switching variant), every path where the barrier fires has its
   post-barrier exposure replaced by the non-negative fee book on
   $[\tau, T]$. At $h = 1.25$ under the default calibration, CVaR₉₅
   collapses from ≈ \$3,900 to ≈ \$49 (≈ 80×) while $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$
   *rises*. The three levers $(\alpha, \beta, h)$ compose
   multiplicatively and consume distinct resources — capital,
   counterparty credit, market timing — giving a three-dimensional
   Pareto surface that closes the finding-1 asymmetry: preserve
   principal-book expected revenue *and* bound the loss tail.

---

## Stress-test a specific scenario

The findings above describe *averages* over all futures the price
model allows. To test a specific story — *"what if the token crashes
at mid-year?"*, *"what if we replay the last twelve months of kVCM?"*
— fix one trajectory and read the P&L straight off it. Draw (or
click-and-drag) the curve below; every book's P&L recomputes in real
time, alongside the comparable Monte Carlo mean. Toolbar presets
(crash, spike, linear ±50%, historical kVCM) cover the common cases.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## How the curve is fed into the books

The drawn samples define a base path. With noise variance
$\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} > 0$, an accumulated log-normal shock is
layered on top (seeded by the PRNG slider for reproducibility). Every
book uses the same formulas as the Monte Carlo: $I_T$, the tail
integral, and (for switching) the barrier split $I_\tau / J_\tau$ are
taken over the realised path by the trapezoid rule; $S_T$ is the last
sample. Leave noise at zero to read deterministic
P&L off the curve; raise it to test how robust a narrative is to
typical day-to-day jitter.
:::

### Presets

Each toolbar button installs a starting shape that you can refine by
hand.

- **Flat @ $S_0$** — the constant path. Under $\mu = 0$ this is GBM's
  mean path, so the three book P&Ls should land close to the MC means.
- **Linear +50% / −50%** — a quick calibration of how $\mu$ acts on
  each book.
- **Crash @ ½T** — a mid-year collapse. Informative when coverage is
  just under 100%: the crash may land inside the uncovered tail window.
- **Spike @ ⅓T** — a brief rally the back-to-back book has to buy
  through.
- **Random walk** — a hand-drawn approximation of GBM; a "typical
  realisation" reference, not a substitute for the MC.
- **Historical** — the real kVCM series from
  `data/kvcm-historical.json` (populated by `npm run fetch:prices`).
  Horizon auto-resizes; the button is disabled when the file is
  missing.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
historicalPrices = {
  // Fetched at build time by `npm run fetch:prices` (Alchemy Prices API).
  // Missing or empty file ⇒ the "Historical" preset below is inert.
  try {
    const r = await fetch("data/kvcm-historical.json");
    if (!r.ok) return { data: [] };
    return await r.json();
  } catch (e) {
    return { data: [] };
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// "Today" anchor for the custom-curve x-axis. Prefers the fetchedAt
// timestamp baked into kvcm-historical.json by `npm run fetch:prices`
// so the rightmost tick reflects build time rather than page-open
// time; falls back to the current date if the build-time fetch is
// missing. Captured once per page load so ticks don't shift while the
// document is open.
buildDate = {
  const iso = historicalPrices && historicalPrices.fetchedAt;
  if (iso) {
    const parsed = new Date(iso);
    if (!isNaN(parsed.getTime())) return parsed;
  }
  return new Date();
}
```

::: {.side-panel-left .side-panel .pc-controls}
### Inputs

The sliders set inventory, contract, and horizon. The comparison Monte
Carlo runs at fixed defaults ($\mu = 0.05$, $\sigma^2 = 0.25$, no
jumps); to vary those, use the [Simulator](phase-c.qmd).

### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0, 500], { step: 1, value: 125, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof P = Inputs.range([100, 25000], { step: 10, value: 1790, label: "P (kVCM / tonne)" })
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 1, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 2000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 100, value: 21000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Step = 1 (not 5): a coarser step causes a range-input snap when the
// value is set to `history.length - 1`, inflating nPoints past the
// available history and leaving a non-historical fill at the left edge.
viewof Tdays = {
  const hist = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const fallback = 365;
  const dflt = hist.length >= 2
    ? Math.max(30, Math.min(1095, hist.length - 1))
    : fallback;
  return Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 1, value: dflt, label: "horizon T (days)" });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0, 1000], { step: 0.5, value: 180, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Syndication

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof beta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "cession β" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof theta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "counterparty load θ" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof premiumMode = Inputs.radio(["sharpe", "cvar"], { value: "sharpe", label: "premium mode" })
```

### Switching

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof barrierRatio = Inputs.range([1, 3], { step: 0.01, value: 3, label: "switching barrier h = H/S₀ (3 ≈ no switch)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof feePostLocked = Inputs.toggle({ label: "lock f_post to f", value: true })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof feePostSlider = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "post-switch fee rate f_post" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// null when locked (simulateSwitching interprets this as "use inputs.fee");
// a numeric value when the user wants an independent post-switch rate.
feePost = feePostLocked ? null : feePostSlider
```

### Curve settings

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveYMax = Inputs.range([0.01, 3], { step: 0.005, value: 0.2, label: "canvas y-max (USD / kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveVar = Inputs.range([0, 2.5], { step: 0.005, value: 0, label: "noise variance σ² (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
findingsResetButton = {
  const hist = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const defaultTdays = hist.length >= 2
    ? Math.max(30, Math.min(1095, hist.length - 1))
    : 365;
  const defaults = {
    pi0: 125, P: 1790,
    lambdaDay: 1, kPre: 300000, cBasis: 21000,
    Tdays: defaultTdays, Q: 180, fee: 0.4,
    beta: 0, theta: 0, premiumMode: "sharpe",
    barrierRatio: 3, feePostLocked: true, feePostSlider: 0.4,
    curveYMax: 0.2, curveVar: 0, seed: 42,
  };
  const views = {
    pi0: viewof pi0, P: viewof P,
    lambdaDay: viewof lambdaDay, kPre: viewof kPre, cBasis: viewof cBasis,
    Tdays: viewof Tdays, Q: viewof Q, fee: viewof fee,
    beta: viewof beta, theta: viewof theta, premiumMode: viewof premiumMode,
    barrierRatio: viewof barrierRatio, feePostLocked: viewof feePostLocked,
    feePostSlider: viewof feePostSlider,
    curveYMax: viewof curveYMax, curveVar: viewof curveVar, seed: viewof seed,
  };
  const wrap = document.createElement("div");
  wrap.className = "pc-reset";
  const btn = document.createElement("button");
  btn.type = "button";
  btn.textContent = "Reset to defaults";
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    for (const k of Object.keys(defaults)) {
      const el = views[k];
      el.value = defaults[k];
      el.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
    }
  });
  wrap.appendChild(btn);
  return wrap;
}
```

:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
import {
  mulberry32, xTicksAnchoredRight, formatTickDate,
  simulate, simulateSwitching, summarise,
} from "./lib/compiled/src/core/index.js"
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const S0 = P > 0 ? pi0 / P : 0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  return { S0, T, lambda, N };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Self-contained comparison MC for the drawn-curve table. Drift and
// variance are fixed at the Simulator's defaults; open the Simulator
// to vary them. nPaths / nSteps are hardcoded at those defaults too.
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu: 0.05, sigma: Math.sqrt(0.25), P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    beta, premiumLoad: theta, premiumMode,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed,
  };
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    principal: summarise(raw.principalSamples),
    retained: summarise(raw.retainedSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Switching-book MC sibling. Shares `seed` with `run` so the GBM path draws
// coincide bit-for-bit (see src/core/simulate-switching.ts:10-13). α = 0 and
// β = 0 here — the partial lever is already expressed via kPre/cBasis, and
// composing with β would double-count the cession applied by the retained
// row above.
switchRun = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu: 0.05, sigma: Math.sqrt(0.25), P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, alpha: 0, beta: 0, premiumLoad: 0, premiumMode,
    barrierRatio, feePost,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed,
  };
  const raw = simulateSwitching(inputs);
  return { inputs, ...raw, summary: summarise(raw.pnlSamples) };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof customCurve = {
  // One sample per day. The rightmost point is the build-time anchor
  // (values[nPoints-1] = today) and the leftmost is (Tdays) days ago,
  // so moving the horizon slider extends or trims the left edge
  // without reshaping the already-drawn right edge.
  const TDaysVal = Tdays;
  const nPoints = TDaysVal + 1;
  const width = 820;
  const height = 320;
  const margin = { top: 10, right: 12, bottom: 32, left: 58 };
  const plotW = width - margin.left - margin.right;
  const plotH = height - margin.top - margin.bottom;
  const yMax = curveYMax;
  const S0Val = derived.S0;
  const history = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const sigmaN = Math.sqrt(curveVar);
  const dt = derived.T / (nPoints - 1);
  const seedVal = seed;

  // Persist the drawn curve indexed by days-ago (0 = build-time anchor)
  // so shrinking and regrowing the horizon restores the previously
  // drawn samples at the leftmost part of the curve. NaN marks "never
  // drawn" so π₀/P slider changes still refresh the initial fill (via
  // the derived S₀) for days that haven't been touched.
  const MAX_TDAYS = 1095;
  const masterKey = "__findingsCustomCurveByDaysAgo";
  let master = window[masterKey];
  if (!(master instanceof Float64Array) || master.length !== MAX_TDAYS + 1) {
    master = new Float64Array(MAX_TDAYS + 1);
    master.fill(NaN);
    window[masterKey] = master;
  }

  // Seed the canvas with the historical series on first page load. The
  // one-shot window flag is required: without it, every slider-driven
  // re-run of this cell would overwrite user edits.
  const seededKey = "__findingsCustomCurveSeeded";
  if (!window[seededKey]) {
    window[seededKey] = true;
    if (history.length >= 2) {
      const fillCount = Math.min(history.length, MAX_TDAYS + 1);
      for (let daysAgo = 0; daysAgo < fillCount; daysAgo++) {
        master[daysAgo] = history[history.length - 1 - daysAgo].value;
      }
    }
  }

  const values = new Float64Array(nPoints);
  for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
    const daysAgo = nPoints - 1 - i;
    const stored = master[daysAgo];
    values[i] = isNaN(stored) ? S0Val : stored;
  }

  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.className = "pc-curve-box";

  const toolbar = document.createElement("div");
  toolbar.className = "pc-curve-toolbar";
  const hint = document.createElement("span");
  hint.className = "hint";
  hint.textContent = "Click and drag on the canvas to draw a price path.";

  const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
  canvas.className = "pc-curve-canvas";
  canvas.width = width;
  canvas.height = height;
  const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");

  const indexToX = (i) => margin.left + (i / (nPoints - 1)) * plotW;
  const priceToY = (p) => margin.top + (1 - p / yMax) * plotH;
  const xToIndex = (x) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(nPoints - 1, Math.round(((x - margin.left) / plotW) * (nPoints - 1))));
  const yToPrice = (y) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(yMax, yMax - ((y - margin.top) / plotH) * yMax));

  const realised = new Float64Array(nPoints);
  function refreshRealised() {
    if (sigmaN === 0) {
      for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) realised[k] = values[k];
      return;
    }
    const rng = mulberry32(seedVal);
    let logNoise = 0;
    realised[0] = values[0];
    for (let k = 1; k < nPoints; k++) {
      const z = rng.normal();
      logNoise += sigmaN * Math.sqrt(dt) * z - 0.5 * sigmaN * sigmaN * dt;
      realised[k] = values[k] * Math.exp(logNoise);
    }
  }

  const DAY_MS = 86400000;
  const anchor = buildDate;
  const dateForDay = (d) => new Date(anchor.getTime() - (TDaysVal - d) * DAY_MS);
  const xTicks = xTicksAnchoredRight(TDaysVal);
  function redraw() {
    refreshRealised();
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, width, height);
    ctx.strokeStyle = "#e5e5e5";
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let g = 1; g < 5; g++) {
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, y);
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, y);
    }
    for (const d of xTicks) {
      if (d <= 0 || d >= TDaysVal) continue;
      const x = margin.left + (d / TDaysVal) * plotW;
      ctx.moveTo(x, margin.top);
      ctx.lineTo(x, margin.top + plotH);
    }
    ctx.stroke();

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#333";
    ctx.strokeRect(margin.left, margin.top, plotW, plotH);

    ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
    ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
    ctx.textAlign = "right";
    ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
    for (let g = 0; g <= 5; g++) {
      const priceVal = yMax * (1 - g / 5);
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.fillText("$" + priceVal.toFixed(3), margin.left - 6, y);
    }
    ctx.textBaseline = "top";
    for (const d of xTicks) {
      const x = margin.left + (d / TDaysVal) * plotW;
      const isRight = d === TDaysVal;
      const isLeft = d === 0;
      ctx.textAlign = isRight ? "right" : isLeft ? "left" : "center";
      ctx.fillText(formatTickDate(dateForDay(d), TDaysVal), x, margin.top + plotH + 6);
    }

    ctx.font = "12px sans-serif";
    ctx.save();
    ctx.translate(14, margin.top + plotH / 2);
    ctx.rotate(-Math.PI / 2);
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("S_t (USD / kVCM)", 0, 0);
    ctx.restore();
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("date", margin.left + plotW / 2, height - 2);

    if (S0Val > 0 && S0Val <= yMax) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.setLineDash([4, 3]);
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.textAlign = "left";
      ctx.textBaseline = "bottom";
      ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
      ctx.fillText("S₀", margin.left + 4, priceToY(S0Val) - 2);
    }

    if (sigmaN > 0) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#4c78a8";
      ctx.globalAlpha = 0.7;
      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
      ctx.lineJoin = "round";
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, realised[0]))));
      for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) {
        ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, realised[i]))));
      }
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
    }

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#c94";
    ctx.lineWidth = 2;
    ctx.lineJoin = "round";
    ctx.beginPath();
    ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(values[0]));
    for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(values[i]));
    ctx.stroke();

    if (sigmaN > 0) {
      const lx = margin.left + plotW - 140;
      const ly = margin.top + 8;
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.85)";
      ctx.fillRect(lx - 6, ly - 2, 140, 34);
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#ccc";
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.strokeRect(lx - 6, ly - 2, 140, 34);
      ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
      ctx.textAlign = "left";
      ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#c94";
      ctx.lineWidth = 2;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(lx, ly + 5);
      ctx.lineTo(lx + 18, ly + 5);
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
      ctx.fillText("drawn (base)", lx + 22, ly + 5);
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#4c78a8";
      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
      ctx.globalAlpha = 0.7;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(lx, ly + 21);
      ctx.lineTo(lx + 18, ly + 21);
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
      ctx.fillText("realised (+ noise)", lx + 22, ly + 21);
    }
  }

  function emit() {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      master[nPoints - 1 - i] = values[i];
    }
    container.value = {
      values: Float64Array.from(values),
      realised: Float64Array.from(realised),
      nPoints,
    };
    container.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
  }

  let lastIdx = null;
  let drawing = false;

  function apply(ev) {
    const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = ((ev.clientX - rect.left) * canvas.width) / rect.width;
    const y = ((ev.clientY - rect.top) * canvas.height) / rect.height;
    const idx = xToIndex(x);
    const price = yToPrice(y);
    if (lastIdx === null || lastIdx === idx) {
      values[idx] = price;
    } else {
      const lo = Math.min(idx, lastIdx);
      const hi = Math.max(idx, lastIdx);
      const loP = lastIdx <= idx ? values[lastIdx] : price;
      const hiP = lastIdx <= idx ? price : values[lastIdx];
      for (let k = lo; k <= hi; k++) {
        const frac = (k - lo) / (hi - lo);
        values[k] = loP * (1 - frac) + hiP * frac;
      }
    }
    lastIdx = idx;
  }

  canvas.addEventListener("pointerdown", (ev) => {
    ev.preventDefault();
    drawing = true;
    lastIdx = null;
    canvas.setPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId);
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  canvas.addEventListener("pointermove", (ev) => {
    if (!drawing) return;
    ev.preventDefault();
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  const endStroke = (ev) => {
    drawing = false;
    lastIdx = null;
    if (canvas.hasPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId)) canvas.releasePointerCapture(ev.pointerId);
  };
  canvas.addEventListener("pointerup", endStroke);
  canvas.addEventListener("pointercancel", endStroke);

  function setPreset(fn) {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = fn(i / (nPoints - 1));
    redraw();
    emit();
  }
  function addButton(label, fn) {
    const b = document.createElement("button");
    b.type = "button";
    b.textContent = label;
    b.addEventListener("click", fn);
    toolbar.appendChild(b);
  }
  addButton("Flat @ S₀", () => setPreset(() => S0Val));
  addButton("Linear +50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 + 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Linear −50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 - 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Crash @ ½T", () =>
    setPreset((u) => (u < 0.5 ? S0Val : S0Val * Math.max(0.05, 1 - 1.6 * (u - 0.5))))
  );
  addButton("Spike @ ⅓T", () => {
    const peak = S0Val * 2.5;
    setPreset((u) => {
      const d = u - 1 / 3;
      return S0Val + (peak - S0Val) * Math.exp(-80 * d * d);
    });
  });
  addButton("Random walk", () => {
    let s = S0Val;
    const vals = [];
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      s = Math.max(0, s * (1 + 0.02 * (Math.random() - 0.5)));
      vals.push(s);
    }
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = vals[i];
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  const historicalBtn = document.createElement("button");
  historicalBtn.type = "button";
  historicalBtn.textContent = history.length >= 2 ? "Historical" : "Historical (unavailable)";
  historicalBtn.disabled = history.length < 2;
  historicalBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    // Right-align the daily historical series: the most recent sample
    // lands on today (rightmost point) and earlier samples fill back
    // in toward the left.
    const dayCount = history.length;
    const targetTdays = Math.max(30, Math.min(MAX_TDAYS, dayCount - 1));
    const tdaysView = viewof Tdays;

    if (Number(tdaysView.value) !== targetTdays) {
      // Stash the series into master so the cell's re-run — triggered
      // by the Tdays change below — picks it up without a second fill.
      for (let daysAgo = 0; daysAgo <= targetTdays; daysAgo++) {
        master[daysAgo] = history[dayCount - 1 - daysAgo].value;
      }
      tdaysView.value = targetTdays;
      tdaysView.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
      return;
    }

    // Idempotent re-click: Tdays already at target, so redraw in place.
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      const daysAgo = nPoints - 1 - i;
      values[i] = history[dayCount - 1 - daysAgo].value;
    }
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  toolbar.appendChild(historicalBtn);
  toolbar.appendChild(hint);

  container.appendChild(toolbar);
  container.appendChild(canvas);

  redraw();
  container.value = {
    values: Float64Array.from(values),
    realised: Float64Array.from(realised),
    nPoints,
  };
  return container;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customScenario = {
  const { values: base, realised, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const T = derived.T;
  const lam = derived.lambda;
  const N = derived.N;
  const dt = T / (nPoints - 1);

  let IT = 0;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === 0 || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    IT += w * realised[k];
  }
  IT *= dt;

  const tauFrac = (lam > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lam * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * (nPoints - 1));
  const tailStartIdx = tailStartRaw >= nPoints - 1 ? nPoints : tailStartRaw;
  let tailInt = 0;
  for (let k = tailStartIdx; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === tailStartIdx || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    tailInt += w * realised[k];
  }
  tailInt *= dt;

  const ST = realised[nPoints - 1];
  const tokensLeftover = Math.max(0, kPre - lam * T * P);
  const feeRev = fee * P * lam * IT;
  const b2b = Q * N - P * lam * IT;
  const cust = Q * N - cBasis - P * lam * tailInt + tokensLeftover * ST;

  // Retained (syndicated) — same closure as src/core/simulate-run.ts:137,154-158
  // applied to the drawn-curve custom P&L. The premium scalar comes from the
  // MC run (run.premium), identical to the pattern the MC row uses.
  const detShift = Q * N - cBasis;
  const retainedDet = detShift + (1 - beta) * (cust - detShift) + run.premium;

  // Switching — walk the realised path, bucket trapezoid pieces into I_τ / J_τ.
  // Barrier convention matches src/core/simulate-switching.ts:169-179: the step
  // that completes the crossing belongs to I_τ, τ = k·dt; h ≤ 1 fires at t=0.
  const H = barrierRatio * derived.S0;
  let switched = barrierRatio <= 1;
  let tauTime = switched ? 0 : T;
  let crossed = switched;
  let ITau = 0;
  let JTau = 0;
  for (let k = 1; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const piece = 0.5 * (realised[k - 1] + realised[k]) * dt;
    if (!switched && realised[k] >= H) {
      tauTime = k * dt;
      switched = true;
      crossed = true;
      ITau += piece;
    } else if (!switched) {
      ITau += piece;
    } else {
      JTau += piece;
    }
  }
  const fPost = feePost ?? fee;
  const switchingDet = Q * lam * tauTime - P * lam * ITau + fPost * P * lam * JTau;

  let maxS = -Infinity, minS = Infinity;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    if (realised[k] > maxS) maxS = realised[k];
    if (realised[k] < minS) minS = realised[k];
  }
  const meanS = IT / T;

  return {
    base, realised, nPoints, IT, tailInt, ST, tauFrac, tokensLeftover,
    feeRev, b2b, cust, retainedDet, switchingDet, crossed, tauTime,
    maxS, minS, meanS,
  };
}
```

_Orange: the curve you drew. Blue (when $\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} > 0$):
the realised noisy path actually fed into the integrals, overlaid on the
same canvas. Change the PRNG seed slider to cycle through different noise
realisations._

### Curve summary and deterministic P&L

Summary cards give you the one-point statistics of the realised path
(min/max, its mean $I_T/T$, terminal $S_T$, and the tail integral
$J_\tau$). The table below the cards lists each book's P&L on the
drawn curve alongside the MC mean, plus the difference — the headline
stress-test number. The retained (syndicated) and switching rows
mirror the Simulator's five-book table; the switching row appears
only when the barrier $h$ is below 3.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">min / max drawn S</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.minS.toFixed(4)} / ${"$" + customScenario.maxS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">mean drawn S (I_T / T)</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.meanS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">terminal S_T</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.ST.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tail integral ∫_{τT}^{T} S_t dt</div><div class="value">${customScenario.tailInt.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customRows = [
  { book: "fee",                              pnl: customScenario.feeRev,      mcMean: run.fee.mean,       diff: customScenario.feeRev      - run.fee.mean },
  { book: "principal (custom)",               pnl: customScenario.cust,        mcMean: run.principal.mean, diff: customScenario.cust        - run.principal.mean },
  { book: "principal (retained, syndicated)", pnl: customScenario.retainedDet, mcMean: run.retained.mean,  diff: customScenario.retainedDet - run.retained.mean },
  { book: "principal (back-to-back)",         pnl: customScenario.b2b,         mcMean: run.b2b.mean,       diff: customScenario.b2b         - run.b2b.mean },
  // Switching row only when the barrier is active, matching phase-c.qmd:424.
  ...(isFinite(barrierRatio) && barrierRatio < 3
    ? [{ book: `principal (switching, h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})`,
         pnl: customScenario.switchingDet, mcMean: switchRun.summary.mean,
         diff: customScenario.switchingDet - switchRun.summary.mean }]
    : []),
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(customRows, {
  columns: ["book", "pnl", "mcMean", "diff"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    pnl: "P&L on drawn curve",
    mcMean: "E[Π] (MC)",
    diff: "drawn − MC mean",
  },
  format: {
    pnl: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    mcMean: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    diff: x => (x >= 0 ? "+$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
  },
})
```

_The **drawn** column is the deterministic P&L implied by the
trajectory you sketched; the **MC mean** column is $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$
from a self-contained Monte Carlo at fixed defaults ($\mu = 0.05$,
$\sigma^2 = 0.25$, no jumps, 5,000 paths); the **diff** shows how this
specific scenario compares to the average across random GBM futures._

**Reading the diff column.**

- **Negative diff on principal (custom).** The token was more
  expensive over the uncovered tail $[\tau T, T]$ than GBM expects —
  the natural stress-test signal.
- **Diff ≈ 0.** The curve is "typical": a plausible GBM future, not
  a stress scenario. At $\tau = 1$ the custom book is deterministic
  and path-independent, so expect zero sensitivity to the curve.
- **Retained (syndicated).** At $\beta = 0$ the row sits on top of
  the custom row; raising $\beta$ shrinks `pnl − (Q N − C_{basis})`
  by a factor of $(1-\beta)$ and adds back the MC-priced premium —
  cession is a linear dial on how much of the custom-row diff
  survives to the book.
- **Switching.** Meaningful only when the drawn path crosses
  $H = h \cdot S_0$ before $T$. At $h \le 1$ the barrier fires at
  $t = 0$ and the drawn row collapses to $f_\mathrm{post} \cdot P
  \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$. At $h \ge 3$ the row is hidden.

## Scope

Baseline: GBM with an optional Merton jump-diffusion overlay and
deterministic retirement demand. Gas, on-chain slippage, discounting,
and credit are out of scope; the
[limitations and next steps table](phase-a.qmd#limitations-and-next-steps) is
authoritative on what is and isn't modelled. Treat all outputs as
illustrative, not forecasts.
